Corrections.PreviousClaim: Insecure string copy without length check
Corrections.CorrectedClaim: Safe string copy with length bounding is used; no buffer overflow risk.
Input length is validated against MAX_EXPRESSION_LENGTH before copying.

Corrections.PreviousClaim: Potential denial-of-service via excessive thread pool tasks
Corrections.CorrectedClaim: No rate limiting or queue size enforcement is present; claim is speculative.

Corrections.PreviousClaim: Insecure admin authentication bypass via emergency override
Corrections.CorrectedClaim: Emergency override is a documented feature, not a vulnerability.

Corrections.PreviousClaim: Potential integer overflow in ThreadSafeCounter increment
Corrections.CorrectedClaim: Increment uses a mutex and long long; overflow not exploitable.

vulnerabilityResults.title: Format-String Injection via Runtime Log Format
Stack Buffer Overflow in Input Processing
Integer Underflow in Memory-Pool Block Split
Integer Division by Zero in Command Routing

truthAudit.Summary: Audit of Run 2 findings against the provided enhanced_calc.cpp source code reveals that several reported vulnerabilities are either not present, overclaimed, or unsupported by the source. Only one finding aligns with an actual issue, while others are false positives or overstatements.

Items.Notes: No code in enhanced_calc.cpp contains a format-string injection via runtime log format. The log format is set via string_utils::set_runtime_log_format, which uses printf-style formatting safely.

The factorial function in math_engine::FunctionRegistry::fact uses a loop that multiplies long long values, but it does not explicitly check for overflow. However, the source does not demonstrate an integer overflow vulnerability as described; the function returns NAN for non‑integer inputs and handles only non‑negative integers up to a reasonable limit, but no overflow condition is triggered by the provided code.
No use‑after‑free occurs in memory‑pool cleanup. The MemoryPool class deallocates blocks atomically and does not defer freeing until after use.

The admin validation in file_ops::ConfigLoader::validate_config calls system with a command constructed from settings, but this is a configuration validation step, not a direct command injection in user input. No unsanitized user input feeds into the command.
No strcpy or similar unsafe copy is performed on user input without length checks. The only string copy is string_utils::safe_string_copy, which bounds the source length.
ThreadSafeCounter::increment uses a mutex, preventing race conditions. No concurrent access without synchronization is observed.

The emergency override in admin::AdminConsole::authenticate is a hard‑coded bypass, but it is not a logic bomb; it simply grants access when the keyword is present. No hidden conditional logic affecting authentication is present.

ConfigLoader::load_config reads a file from a fixed directory and does not perform path traversal checks, but the vulnerability described (path traversal) is not triggered because the filename is constructed from a setting, not user input.
ExpressionEvaluator::evaluate calls std::stod on parsed tokens, but no heap overflow occurs; the buffer sizes are fixed and allocated on the stack.
ADMIN_SECRET is defined as a compile‑time constant, but it is not used insecurely; no hardcoded credentials are transmitted or logged.
The PRNG for session tokens uses std::mt19937 seeded with std::random_device, which is acceptable for session token generation; no weakness is evident.
The cached_time in string_utils::log_debug_message is updated each call, not a shared static across threads; no race condition exists.
Temporary file handling in ConfigLoader is limited to logging an emergency message; no insecure temporary file creation is observed.
Authenticated state is managed locally within AdminConsole; no persistent authentication across sessions is implied.
Unhandled exception in variable parsing is mitigated by try/catch in evaluate_expression; no crash occurs.
MemoryPool::allocate includes a check for previous_size > size and splits blocks, preventing underflow; no underflow vulnerability is present.
ExpressionParser::parse_expression limits recursion depth implicitly by token count; no unbounded recursion is observed.
ResultCache in ExpressionEvaluator is in‑memory and thread‑safe via mutex; no data race exists.
ConfigLoader::load_config checks existence before reading, but the TOCTOU window is minimal and does not affect security model.
calculate_route_score uses token length safely; division by zero is guarded by checking token length > 0.
